evals.prompt: I'm working a $200k enterprise deal selling our cloud data platform to Meridian Financial (Fortune 500 insurance company). The CIO is our executive sponsor, there's a 3-person engineering team doing a technical evaluation, and procurement has already asked for security docs. We're in the proposal stage with a 3-month timeline to close. I've got a pitch deck, two case studies (one in financial services), and a draft pricing proposal. Set up a deal room in Qwilr to organize everything and keep all stakeholders aligned.
evals.expected_output: A complete deal room architecture with hub page and sub-pages tailored to the three stakeholder groups (executive, technical, procurement). Should include hub page copy, content briefs for each sub-page, a mutual action plan with enterprise-appropriate milestones, and an analytics strategy for tracking each stakeholder's engagement.

assertions.name: has_hub_page
assertions.description: Designs a hub page with navigation to sub-pages, welcome content, and key contacts

assertions.name: has_stakeholder_pages
assertions.description: Creates sub-pages tailored to CIO (executive summary), technical team, and procurement

assertions.name: has_mutual_action_plan
assertions.description: Includes a mutual action plan with milestones, owners, and target dates

assertions.name: has_analytics_strategy
assertions.description: Maps which engagement signals to watch per stakeholder type

evals.prompt: We're expanding our existing contract with a customer. They've been using our basic plan for a year and we want to upsell them to enterprise. I need a deal room to present the expansion.
evals.expected_output: An expansion-focused deal room that leads with results and value delivered in year one, then presents the expansion opportunity. Structure should differ from a new-logo deal room — heavier on proving existing value, lighter on company intro and security.

assertions.name: leads_with_results
assertions.description: Hub page and structure lead with value delivered in year one, not company intro

assertions.name: expansion_focused
assertions.description: Content briefs focus on the expansion opportunity and incremental value

assertions.name: adapted_structure
assertions.description: Removes or reduces pages that aren't needed for expansion (e.g., security review)

evals.prompt: Set up a deal room for a partnership deal. We sell an inventory management SaaS and we're co-selling with Deloitte (our channel partner) to land a $350k deal with Titan Manufacturing, a 2,000-person industrial company. Deloitte handles the implementation and change management, we provide the platform. I have a joint pitch deck, our standard proposal, and Deloitte has their own SOW. Both teams need access to shared materials, but we also need separate partner-facing pages with margin details that the customer shouldn't see.
evals.expected_output: A partner-oriented deal room with pages for both the end customer and the channel partner. Should include partner-specific content like co-selling materials and margin details alongside customer-facing pages.

assertions.name: has_partner_page
assertions.description: Includes partner-facing content with co-selling materials

assertions.name: dual_audience
assertions.description: Clearly separates partner-facing and customer-facing content
